title: Reaction rate — the ledger in time
definition: The **reaction rate** is how fast the reaction extent advances — the time derivative of the species ledger. For a reactant A with coefficient $a$, rate $= -\tfrac{1}{a}\tfrac{d[\mathrm{A}]}{dt}$; it is the same $d\xi/dt$ that stoichiometry, thermochemistry, and equilibrium all view differently — here watched *in time*. A rate is **positive and changes as the reaction proceeds** (usually slowing as reactants deplete). How the rate depends on concentration is not read off the balanced equation — it is the empirically measured **rate law** (see [[rate-law]]). Equilibrium is the limit where the forward and reverse rates become equal, so the ledger stops moving.
